edges.relation: Cyclotomic classes provide structured initial blocks for the GS difference family search
GA optimizes the SDS block selection that feeds into the GS array construction
Lattice sieving provides a systematic enumeration method for the same SDS search space
RL learns heuristic policies for the same sequential SDS block construction problem
Cocyclic matrices over Goethals-Seidel loops generalize the GS construction algebraically
Both use algebraic structures (cohomology/curves) to impose orthogonality constraints on matrices
Both model orthogonality as energy minimization in an Ising-type Hamiltonian, classical vs. quantum
Tensor networks simulate quantum annealing classically; both exploit entanglement/correlation structure
Both are learning-based search methods; GA uses population evolution while RL uses policy gradient
GA population diversity mirrors replica diversity in parallel tempering; both combat premature convergence
BP is the message-passing analog of tensor network contraction on tree-like factor graphs
Kronecker product structure is a rank-1 tensor decomposition; tensor networks generalize to higher ranks
Both exploit finite field arithmetic; AG codes evaluate functions while cyclotomy partitions the multiplicative group
BP computes marginals of the same Gibbs distribution that spin glass MC samples from
Both iteratively refine solutions; BP through message passing, QA through quantum tunneling
Cyclotomic structure constrains the lattice to a lower-dimensional subspace, making sieving tractable
Kronecker scaffold provides starting point; GS-based local repair fixes orthogonality defects
RL can learn to select which rows to repair via BP, combining global strategy with local inference
Lattice techniques from coding theory (decoding AG codes) transfer to the SDS enumeration problem
Spin glass relaxation refines the approximate Kronecker-product matrix toward exact orthogonality

bridge_chains.name: algebraic_to_computational
bridge_chains.description: Starts from pure algebraic structure (cyclotomic classes), encodes into combinatorial constraints (GS array), and progressively applies more sophisticated computational search methods (GA then RL)

bridge_chains.name: physics_inspired_optimization
bridge_chains.description: Traverses the physics-information theory bridge: classical spin glass -> quantum annealing -> tensor network representation -> message-passing inference

bridge_chains.name: structural_scaffold_refinement
bridge_chains.description: Uses algebraic geometry to build initial structure, Kronecker products to scaffold it, physics-based optimization to refine globally, and BP for targeted local repair

bridge_chains.name: algebraic_topology_to_computation
bridge_chains.description: Pure algebraic classification (cohomology) -> algebraic construction (AG codes) -> computational enumeration (lattice sieving) -> explicit matrix construction (GS array)

bridge_chains.name: learning_guided_algebraic_search
bridge_chains.description: ML methods progressively incorporate algebraic structure: RL discovers heuristics -> GA exploits diversity -> GS provides framework -> cyclotomy constrains search -> lattice methods enumerate systematically
